title: Deutsch Krummelpfannkuchen
description: A German-inspired sweet bread cobbler featuring fresh basil leaves and a crispy crumb topping.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine yeast and warm milk. Let it sit for 5-7 minutes until foamy.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, and salt. Add the melted butter and mix until combined.
Add the eggs and yeast mixture to the dry ingredients. Mix until a smooth dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10-12 minutes until elastic.
Transfer the dough to a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
In a small saucepan, combine 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter, brown sugar, and 2 tablespoons of granulated sugar. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves.
Pour the sugar mixture into a 9x13-inch baking dish. Arrange the chopped basil leaves on top.
Punch down the dough and roll it out to a 1/4-inch thickness. Place the dough over the sugar mixture.
Mix together oat flour, confectioners' sugar, and a pinch of salt for the crumb topping. Sprinkle over the dough.
Bake for 40-45 minutes or until golden brown.
Let the cobbler cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
